en: like a perfectly natural thing to do, because in my family, reading was the primary group
ko: 엄마가 제 가방에 책을 가득 채워 넣어주셨는데, 저한테는 완전히 자연스러운 일이었어요. 우리 가족에게 독서는 주된 단체

en: activity. And this might sound antisocial to you, but for us, it was really just a different
ko: 활동이었거든요. 비사교적으로 들릴 수 있지만, 우리한테는 그냥 다른 방식의 사교 활동이었어요. 가족이 바로 옆에 앉아 있는 따뜻한 온기를 느끼면서도

en: way of being social. You had the animal warmth of your family sitting right next to you,
but you were also free to go roaming around the adventure land inside your own mind. And
ko: 동시에 자기 마음속 모험의 나라를 자유롭게 돌아다닐 수 있었죠. 저는 캠프도

en: I had this idea that camp was going to be just like this, but better. I had a vision
of 10 girls sitting in a cabin, cozily reading books in their matching nightgowns. Camp was
more like a keg party without any alcohol. And on the very first day, our counselor gathered
ko: 실제 캠프는 술 없는 파티 같은 분위기였죠.

en: us all together, and she taught us a cheer that she said we would be doing every day
ko: 첫날, 상담 선생님이 우리를 다 모아놓고 여름 내내 매일 할 거라는 응원 구호를 가르쳐 주셨어요.

en: for the rest of the summer to instill camp spirit. And it went like this. R-O-W-D-I-E,
